question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findEvmAddressesByIds()] Can an unprivileged user craft a record file where every transaction references a different synthetic contract log with a unique entity ID, causing the evmCache LoadingCache to call loadAll() with a set of 30000 unique IDs, triggering findEvmAddressesByIds() with a 30000-element IN clause that causes PostgreSQL to allocate a large work_mem sort buffer and OOM-kill the DB process?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: updateContractType()] Can an unprivileged user submit transactions that cause updateContractType() to be called with an Iterable<Long> containing duplicate IDs, causing PostgreSQL to perform redundant row-level lock acquisitions and release cycles, amplifying lock contention on the entity table and causing a non-network DoS of the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByAlias()] Can an unprivileged user submit transactions with alias byte arrays that are exactly 35 bytes (ECDSA secp256k1 alias length), causing EntityIdServiceImpl to route them through findByAlias() and then findByAliasEvmAddress() as a fallback, doubling the number of DB queries per transaction and exhausting the DB connection pool?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByAlias()] Can an unprivileged user submit transactions with 35-byte alias values that fail aliasToEvmAddress() conversion, causing findByAliasEvmAddress() to call Utility.handleRecoverableError() for each lookup, flooding the importer error log and exhausting disk I/O, causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findEvmAddressesByIds()] Can an unprivileged user craft transactions that cause the parserContext.getEvmAddressLookupIds() set to grow unboundedly within a single record file processing cycle, causing findEvmAddressesByIds() to be called with an Iterable larger than MAX_CACHE_LOAD_ENTRIES=30000, triggering the batching loop in getCacheMisses() to issue multiple large IN-clause queries that collectively exhaust DB CPU?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByEvmAddress()] Can an unprivileged user submit transactions with EVM addresses that are valid 20-byte values but correspond to entities with deleted=NULL (not deleted=true), causing findByEvmAddress() with 'deleted <> true' to return empty (since NULL <> true is NULL/false in SQL), triggering repeated unnecessary DB queries and cache misses that exhaust the DB connection pool?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByAlias()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the NULL-handling difference in 'deleted <> true' (which excludes NULL rows) versus 'deleted is not true' to cause findByAlias() to return empty for entities with deleted=NULL, forcing the importer to repeatedly re-query the DB for the same alias and exhaust the connection pool?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: updateContractType()] Can an unprivileged user submit a stream of contract-related transactions that causes updateContractType() to be called in rapid succession with large ID lists, causing the @Transactional context to hold open multiple concurrent write transactions on the entity table, exhausting PostgreSQL's max_connections and causing the importer to fail with connection acquisition timeouts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findEvmAddressesByIds()] Can an unprivileged user craft a record file with synthetic contract logs referencing entity IDs that are all cache hits in the evmCache, but then submit a second record file with the same IDs evicted from cache, causing repeated full DB reloads via findEvmAddressesByIds() that exhaust DB CPU and stall the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByAlias()] Can an unprivileged user submit transactions with alias values that are valid protobuf-encoded public keys of maximum size, causing findByAlias() to pass a very large byte[] to the native SQL query, triggering PostgreSQL to allocate large temporary memory for bytea comparison and exhausting shared memory, causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findEvmAddressesByIds()] Can an unprivileged user craft transactions that cause the Caffeine evmCache in SyntheticLogListener to be invalidated between record file processing cycles, forcing a full reload of all entity EVM addresses via findEvmAddressesByIds() on every record file, causing sustained high DB load that stalls the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: updateContractType()] Can an unprivileged user submit transactions that cause updateContractType() to be called with an empty Iterable<Long>, and if the @Modifying query does not short-circuit on empty input, cause PostgreSQL to execute 'update entity set type = CONTRACT where id in ()' which may result in a syntax error or full table scan depending on the JDBC driver behavior, crashing the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByAlias()] Can an unprivileged user submit transactions with alias values that are all-zero byte arrays of various lengths, causing findByAlias() to issue queries that match no index entries and perform full sequential scans of the entity table, exhausting DB CPU and stalling the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByEvmAddress()] Can an unprivileged user submit transactions with evm_address values that are all-zero 20-byte arrays, causing findByEvmAddress() to issue queries that match no index entries and perform full sequential scans of the entity table, exhausting DB CPU and stalling the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findEvmAddressesByIds()] Can an unprivileged user craft transactions that cause the same entity ID to appear in the evmAddressLookupIds set millions of times across many record files, causing repeated findEvmAddressesByIds() calls with the same ID, exhausting DB connection pool resources and stalling the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: updateContractType()] Can an unprivileged user submit transactions that cause updateContractType() to be called with a list of IDs that do not exist in the entity table, causing PostgreSQL to perform a full index scan for each non-existent ID, exhausting DB CPU and stalling the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByAlias()] Can an unprivileged user submit transactions with alias values that are valid ECDSA secp256k1 public keys (35 bytes) but whose derived EVM address matches an existing entity, causing findByAlias() to miss and findByAliasEvmAddress() to hit, but the double-lookup pattern per transaction exhausts the DB connection pool under high transaction volume?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByEvmAddress()] Can an unprivileged user submit a high volume of contract call transactions referencing unique EVM addresses, causing findByEvmAddress() to be called for each transaction without cache hits, saturating the DB connection pool and causing the importer to stall with connection timeout errors?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findEvmAddressesByIds()] Can an unprivileged user craft a record file where synthetic contract logs reference entity IDs that span the full range of Long values (including negative IDs), causing findEvmAddressesByIds() to issue a query with an IN clause containing extreme Long values that confuse the PostgreSQL query planner and cause a full sequential scan?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: updateContractType()] Can an unprivileged user submit transactions that cause updateContractType() to be called concurrently from multiple importer threads with overlapping ID sets, causing PostgreSQL deadlocks that force transaction rollbacks, causing the importer to enter an infinite retry loop and halt record file processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByAlias()] Can an unprivileged user submit transactions with alias values that are exactly 1 byte long, causing findByAlias() to issue queries that cannot use any index on the alias column (if the index is partial or length-filtered), resulting in full sequential scans that exhaust DB CPU and stall the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByEvmAddress()] Can an unprivileged user submit transactions with evm_address values that are exactly 1 byte long, causing findByEvmAddress() to issue queries that cannot use any index on the evm_address column, resulting in full sequential scans that exhaust DB CPU and stall the importer?
